question_understood: Resolve three Sev 2b/3 prose_coverage escalations (esc-001 section charters uncovered; esc-002 contract files uncovered; esc-003 Discovery assumptions A1/A3/A4/A9 + 7 ledger-OOS + 19 section-OOS + chosen_verb uncovered) with minimum disruption to the verified integrated build.
key_facts: 28/28 harness assertions passing including PNV.1 against the integrated bundle.
All 13 MCAs physically verified by Critic against the integrated artifact.
All three escalations are explicitly non-blocking and explicitly diagnosed as covers-pointer (schema-graph) gaps, NOT behavioral verification gaps.

Each escalation's own context paragraph identifies an existing assertion that entails the uncovered claim (e.g., AA.S1.3 already exercises C-S1-S2 and C-S1-S3; MCA.TDIPA.* entails A1; DCA.OOS.TELEMETRY.1 entails A9; PNV.1 references chosen_verb='display').
constraints: Do not require code changes (build is verified end-to-end).
Preserve S1-S6 acceptance assertions and MCAs untouched in behavior.
Questioning authority granted: may flag framing concern if briefing misdiagnoses.
candidate_resolution_directions: A: widen 'covers' fields on existing assertions to multi-target arrays pointing at the uncovered schema paths (pure JSON-graph patch, no behavior change)
B: add new charter/contract/OOS coverage assertions that duplicate existing scenarios but point covers at the gap targets
C: accept the gaps via Arbiter Principle-C strict-pass waiver; record rationale in arbitration log only
D: introduce a thin coverage_aliases sidecar (adapter layer) mapping gap schema-pointers to the assertions that entail them — sections file untouched
framing_check: Briefing framing matches Critic's own diagnosis: the gap is in covers-pointer wiring, not in behavior. Framing appears sound.
